content.summaryText: Acute Pediatric Respiratory Symptoms in Common Pediatric Illnesses focuses on pediatric triage with dehydration and respiratory red-flag detection. Primary objective: Recognize work-of-breathing signs.

content.questionText: In Acute Pediatric Respiratory Symptoms, which action best supports 'Recognize work-of-breathing signs'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Use age-based assessment and parental symptom timeline
Using adult thresholds for pediatric vital signs
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: use age-based assessment and parental symptom timeline. Practical emphasis: Prioritize oxygen support.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when respiratory distress, altered consciousness, persistent high fever.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
